flags: Multi-thread to power: This deal is dangerously single-threaded on Cindy Simons (Manager, Procurement, Evaluator), who has no budget authority and went silent after the demo. Cindy explicitly named the CFO and corporate IT as the decision authorities on 28 January 2026. The next move is to secure a joint discovery call with Cindy, the CFO, and corporate IT (by mid-July 2026) to map the buying committee, validate the quality document pain and cost justification, assess SAP integration complexity, and identify the Economic Buyer. Until power is engaged, this deal cannot advance.

Land the SAP wedge with ROI: SAP is the entrenched incumbent (migrated March 2025), and Cindy questioned the value of Zycus over SAP on the 28 January call. The wedge is SAP's high expansion cost and lack of purpose-built supplier quality document management (the pain Cindy owns). However, the wedge was not quantified or landed. The next move is to run a lightweight ROI workshop with Cindy and the CFO (by late July 2026) that quantifies the time and cost pain of manual quality document tracking (15+ docs per vendor, overwhelmed team, email/Excel chaos) versus the cost of SAP expansion, and presents a scoped pilot (quality document management module only, 30-60 day proof-of-value) to demonstrate fast ROI and reduce change-management risk. This positions Zycus as a low-risk, high-value complement to SAP, not a rip-and-replace.
